revision.comment: Bot: mark 3 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町横室)]] == Overview == {{Infobox religious building | 名称 = Akagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Fujimi Town Yokomuro|ja|富士見町横室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|1=Fujimimachiyokomuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126188}} | deity = {{ill|Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | type = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | 緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 15.4 | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 3 | 経度秒 = 48.4 | image = File:Akagi-jinja FujimimachiYokomuro Mebashi.jpg | 創建 = Unknown | location = {{ill|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.454278|N|139.063444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Akagi Shrine''' (赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Fujimi Town Yokomuro|ja|富士見町横室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|1=Fujimimachiyokomuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126188}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. Its former Rank under the old shrine classification system was that of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088244/1/532 明治神社誌料：府県郷社 上]』{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|pp=1071-1072|qq=}}. The shrine building stands atop Mount Jūni. == History == The exact origins are unknown, but it is said to have been established as the {{ill|satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satomiya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (village shrine) of {{ill|Ōtō Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町赤城山)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Akagi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Akagi Shrine|1=Daidō Akagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635469}} and was venerated by the lords of {{ill|Maebashi Castle|en|Maebashi Castle|qid=Q10898917|lt=Maebashi Castle|1=Maebashi Castle}} as a guardian of the {{ill|kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kimon|qq=}} (demon gate) alongside the Ōtō Akagi Shrine{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|pp=1071-1072|qq=}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine){{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1979|pp=1142-1143|qq=}}. The enshrined deities are: - {{ill|Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} - {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|大日孁命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} - {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} - {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} - {{ill|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|1=Futsunushi}} - {{ill|Okutsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|奥津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550732}} - {{ill|Ukemochi no Kami|ja|保食命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2280323}} - {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} - {{ill|Okutsuhime no Mikoto|ja|奥津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550733}} - {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} - {{ill|Tatsuta-hime|ja|竜田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550734}} - {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火結命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Traces of a {{ill|tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (pit-style stone chamber) and a circular arrangement of river stones remain within the shrine grounds, and the discovery of artifacts such as swords and {{ill|mimikazari|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mimikazari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mimikazari|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11647642}} (earrings) suggests the presence of a burial mound. This site is not registered in the Jōmō Burial Mound Survey{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=665|qq=}}. == Mount Jūni == [[File:Juuni-yama.jpg|thumb|249x249px|Mount Jūni seen from the southwest]] '''Mount Jūni''' (十二山) is a hilly area spanning from {{ill|Fujimi Town Yokomuro|ja|富士見町横室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Yokomuro|1=Fujimimachiyokomuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126188}} to {{ill|Fujimi Town Hikida|ja|富士見町引田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Hikida|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi Town Hikida|1=Fujimi Town Hikida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126137}} in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Kunimigatake'''{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=471|qq=}} or '''Kunimigaoka'''{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1979|p=1141|qq=}}, with an elevation of 300 meters{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|pp=1071-1072|qq=}}. It is famous for azaleas and is mentioned in the local karuta game "Fujimi Karuta" with the line: "Azaleas of Akagi and Mount Jūni"{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|pp=1071-1072|qq=}}{{Cite web |title=Fujimi Karuta Tour, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture |url=https://fujimimachi.jp/karuta/karuta_tu.html |website=fujimimachi.jp |access-date=2024-07-18|qq=}}. The name is believed to derive from the worship of the mountain god {{ill|Jūnisho Shrine|ja|十二所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnisho Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnisho Shrine|qq=}} (Ōyamatsumi) as "Jūninyō"{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=1009|qq=}}. The Edo-period "{{ill|Maebashi Fudoki|ja|前橋風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" states that the name originates from the worship of the Twelve Heavenly Deities at the mountain summit『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1183477/1/34 前橋風土記]』. There is a legend that stones were quarried from Mount Jūni during the construction of Maebashi Castle{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=1039|qq=}}. At the summit is a stone {{ill|Kōshin-tō|ja|庚申塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-tō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-tō|qq=}} established in the first year of Man'en (1860){{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|pp=1071-1072|qq=}}, and halfway up the mountain is a {{ill|Gorintō|en|Gorintō|qid=Q304906|lt=Gorintō|1=Gorintō}} called Kazefusama{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=1015|qq=}}. To the southwest foot is a {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|en|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qid=Q17004306|lt=Batō Kannon|1=Hayagriva (Buddhism)}}, which is said to be the remains of a {{ill|jishibai|ja|地芝居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jishibai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jishibai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 素人歌舞伎|1=amateur kabuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605564}} (folk theater) stage{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=1036|qq=}}. On the southern slope lies a round burial mound, 35 meters in diameter, called the '''Hatsumuro Kofun''' (Jōmō Burial Mound Survey No. 7). It contains a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, but has been plundered{{Sfn|Fujimi Village History Compilation Committee|1954|p=665|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=富士見村誌 |date=1954-11-23 |year=1954 |publisher=富士見村役場 |ref=harv |editor=富士見村誌編纂委員会|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=富士見村誌 続編 |date=1979-10-1 |year=1979 |publisher=富士見村役場 |ref=harv |editor=富士見村誌編纂委員会|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kujūku-yama|ja|九十九山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku-yama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku-yama|1=Kujūku-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131903886}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ|Geography of Maebashi|Mountains of Gunma Prefecture|Mountain name list|Mount Akagi|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町横室)|version=101161972|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町横室)]] {{wikidata link|Q131275527}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Maebashi]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
